title: fraxinus americana var. caroliniana
intro: Fraxinus americana var. caroliniana, also known as the Carolina ash, is a variety of the white ash tree that is native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Fraxinus americana var. caroliniana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and provide relief from digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant's bark, leaves, and roots can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
